feedback.text: I would like to suggest to the European Commission to broaden the scope of the Digital Fairness Act to also protect video games from intentional destruction by their publishers after sale. I acknowledge no publisher of a game can be made responsible for supporting a product indefinitely, it is absolutely in the realm of possibility to remove the components that require an online connection and leave the offline aspects of a game intact. It is not unreasonable to expect that a product that I paid for should still be able to function, at least partially, when the original publisher ceases to maintain it. In many cases however, the publisher of a video game chooses intentionally to disable all functionality, taking away access to a game I paid for with no recourse of action. I therefore humbly request for you to include the protection video games into the Digital Fairness Act.
